references.source: NHS digital service manual design system
references.borrowed: Use a consistent accessible service component vocabulary and plain transactional structure.
references.rejected: Did not import NHS-branded visual chrome because the local staff shell owns product styling.

references.borrowed: Button copy names the specific action and uses smaller inline-style controls only where scoped to a nearby target.
references.rejected: No warning-button styling because insert is disabled rather than destructive-confirmed when blocked.

references.borrowed: Buttons must make sense out of context and color cannot be the only signifier for blocked state.
references.rejected: No custom error summary because blocked insert reasons are local to each section, not a form submission.

references.borrowed: Keyboard guidance informed Home, End, and arrow-key expectations for moving among related compare/draft controls.
references.rejected: Did not implement full tab roles for compare mode because all three views are low-latency buttons inside one card, not separate tab panels.

references.borrowed: Roving focus approach for draft cards and predictable keyboard movement inside the composite section deck.
references.rejected: No aria-activedescendant because real card focus gives clearer browser focus rings and simpler Playwright assertions.

references.borrowed: Use locator-scoped ARIA snapshots for deck and representative card semantics.
references.rejected: No broad full-page snapshot because the active workspace has unrelated route chrome.

references.borrowed: Use element `toHaveScreenshot()` baselines for deterministic rail states.
references.rejected: No page-level screenshot baseline because unrelated queue and task regions are outside 419 scope.

references.borrowed: Capture traces around blocked insert and visual proof so before/after DOM states are debuggable.
references.rejected: No trace attachment assertions in production code; traces remain test artifacts only.

references.borrowed: Low-noise actions should be available through consistent visible controls and keyboard patterns.
references.rejected: No command-menu shortcut layer because 419 needs direct target-bound insert controls.

references.borrowed: Dense information should use consistent row/card heights, concise headings, and restrained toolbar action count.
references.rejected: No actual table component because draft sections are independently actionable cards.

references.borrowed: Slot and lease labels stay concise, compact, and mostly read-only.
references.rejected: No interactive tag behavior because target-slot pills are context labels, not filters.

references.borrowed: Persistent layout guidance supports keeping the rail shell mounted while route-specific draft content changes.
references.rejected: No Next.js-specific implementation because this workspace app is Vite, but the shell-continuity principle applies.
